evidence.excerpt:
Big Bytes Pizza
 “Everybody knows there's nothing like a delicious slice of Big Bytes Pizza. And it's perfect, just before On-Lyne hits the stage. My favorite bite? Pepperoni with extra cheese, of course. What's yours?

 Let's connect, over a slice of Big Bytes Pizza!”

 — Zeke from On-Lyne

 Big Bytes Pizza is a popular pizza chain in Höllvania .

[ edit | edit source ]
 On the official site , near the bottom of the page where the three images are located, you can see a tomato sticking out from behind the rightmost image. Clicking on the tomato produces a sound, and clicking anywhere after that will throw and splatter a tomato centered on where you clicked. Doing so on the image of a pizza slice near the top of the menu (The one with five pepperoni slices on it) will reveal an image depicting the molecular structure Guanine (G) , one of the four bases for DNA.

 Images located at the bottom of the Big Bytes Pizza website. Note the tomato sticking out from under the rightmost image.
 The pizza image from the Big Bytes Pizza website. Splattered tomatoes reveal an image depicting the molecular structure for Guanine.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • The official site includes the text and a phone number, "Call to order today! 1-800-555-2983". When this number is dialed on the phone in the site for the City Wide Metro , it will play a jingle from the song " Tarantella Napoletana ", which is associated with Italy and pizza in popular media.

 Update 29.10 (2021-03-19)

 • Removed from drop tables.

 We wanted to reduce the number of Resources we introduced in our original Railjack launch. They'll remain in your Inventory as we’ll be looking at possibly reintroducing them at a later date. All Blueprints that required these items have been updated to reflect viable Resources, and respective drops have been swapped to Ticor Plate where appropriate. These Resources have also been removed from Helminths Bile Feeding menu. If you spot any mentions of these Resources that we might have missed please let us know!

 For Tenno who have partially contributed these Resources to Wreckage, the percentage of your contribution will be honoured for the Resource that replaces it. Ex: If you contributed 60% of the needed Copernics for a Carcinnox, that 60% contribution will be carried over when the Blueprints are updated.

 • Salacia , Neptune is the recommended node for gathering Cryptographic ALU, yielding approximately 12+ per run without a Resource Booster .

• Utilizing Itzal 's third ability, Cosmic Crush , allows easy acquisition of ALU as it pulls in pickups to your location while killing enemies.

• When dropped by enemies, Cryptographic ALUs look identical to Omega Isotopes . Due to their dark color, this can make them difficult to make out against the backdrop of space.

• The Steel Path 's resource drop chance bonus increases Cryptographic ALU's drop chance.

• Equipping a Beast companion and the mod Resourceful Retriever will increase the chance of doubling Cryptographic ALU by 18%

[ edit | edit source ]
 • ALU stands for Arithmetic Logic Unit , an electronic circuit that is a fundamental building block in many computing circuits.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • Oxylus ' Botanist will automatically loot plants within range.

• Pallas , Ceres will always spawn two of these in the green water next to spawn.

• Ker , Ceres has a guaranteed large reactor room that will spawn around 4 of these.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • Sarracenia is a genus of Pitcher plants commonly known as Trumpet Pitchers, and are endemic to the North American continent. It is unknown if the Dusklight Sarracenia has any biological relation to the Lunar Pitcher .

• Amaryn's hint about the location of this flower mentions it grows in "100% filth", which she concludes by wondering what kind of flower grows in those conditions. This is a likely reference to darkSector , wherein a character describes the Lotus flower as a "rare beauty [that] blooms among filth and decay".

[ edit | edit source ]

 Update 39.0 (2025-06-25)

 • Players will no longer have to scan plants to earn their extracts.
 • Now, plants like Moonlight Dragonlily and Ruk’s Claw will have a “Hold to Collect” context action to pick them up in-mission.

• Oxylus’ Botanist Precept has been changed to automatically pick any plants within 50m over 2s (at max rank).

• This also applies to the Silver Grove Quest, which requires players to collect certain plant resources to progress. The objective descriptions have been updated to specify “gathering” instead of “scanning”.

• The scanning mechanic was overly complicated for new players, so this simplification should benefit Plant Extract gathering for Tenno across the board.

 Update 32.3 (2023-02-15)

 • Fixed being unable to scan a Dusklight Sarracenia found in the Grineer Shipyard Tileset.

 Update: The Silver Grove (2016-08-19)

Genetic Code Template Untradeable Description Copies the dominant genes of a pet onto the template.
This can then be used to increase the chances that traits from the original pet will carry across to a newborn clone. General Information Type Component Sell Price Cannot Sell Introduced Update 14.0 (2014-07-18) Official Drop Tables https://www.warframe.com/droptables Maintenance 📝 Update Infobox Data
 This page redirects from Imprint . For the lore fragments, see Synthesis#Imprints .
 A Genetic Code Template is a medium used for saving genetic information of a living companion . Pet imprints can be traded or used for a combined incubation.

 Imprints are primarily used as "parents" for a baby pet. By applying two Genetic Code Templates before the incubation phase, the traits of each imprint will be passed onto the new Kubrow or Kavat . Note that imprints do not substitute the normally associated components for incubating a beast; it will still cost you a Kubrow Egg or 10 Kavat Genetic Code on top of the imprints.

 Players can carry up to a maximum of 200 stored imprints.

• 1 Market and Manufacturing

• 2 Imprinting Genetic Information

• 3 Stored Information

• 4 Template Identification

• 5 When Breeding A New Pet

• 6 Trivia

• 7 Patch History

• 8 References

[ edit | edit source ]
 The blueprint can be bought for 50,000 Credits 50,000 and is re-usable indefinitely on all platforms.

 A crafted Genetic Code Template can also be bought on the Market for 15 Platinum 15 .

[ edit | edit source ]
 A Genetic Code Template with an imprint
 When constructed, Genetic Code Templates are empty. Tenno can use them to store the genetic information of an active pet anytime after it has matured. Imprinting a single genetic code for a pet will take 1.5 hours, but can be rushed for 10 Platinum 10 . Up to three imprints can be taken of a single Kavat, and up to two of a single Kubrow. Note that your pet will be unusable while imprinting them, but are usable as normal after the process is over and the finished imprint has been claimed from the incubator.

[ edit | edit source ]
 The traits mentioned below are stored by a Genetic Code template, with each trait detail having a 50%-per-template weighting when used to create a descending pet. Bear in mind that the information stored will only refer to the pet's original appearance, free of any changes to its coat colors or pattern.

[ edit | edit source ]
 A Genetic Code Template contains the name of the pet it was created from and an icon indicating which type of pet it is.

 Huras Kubrow
Prowling stance. Reflecting that the Huras is orientated around stalking.

 Raksa Kubrow
Sitting in a loyal stoic pose. Reflecting Raksa specialty of protecting their master.

 Sahasa Kubrow
Digging pose. Indicating Sahasa's role in digging up items

 Sunika Kubrow
Aggresive pouncing pose. Indicating Sunika's role as an attack Kubrow.

 Chesa Kubrow
Retrieving pose. Indicating Chesa's trait of fetching items.

 Smeeta Kavat
Dashing while turning invisible. Indicating Smeeta's specialty of trickery.

 Adarza Kavat
Facing a reflection of itself. Indicating Adarza's specialty of reflection.

 Vasca Kavat
Facing forward with its body lowered and tail up.

sections.text: This page redirects from Imprint. For the lore fragments, see Synthesis#Imprints. A Genetic Code Template is a medium used for saving genetic information of a living companion. Pet imprints can be traded or used for a combined incubation. Imprints are primarily used as "parents" for a baby pet. By applying two Genetic Code Templates before the incubation phase, the traits of each imprint will be passed onto the new Kubrow or Kavat. Note that imprints do not substitute the normally associated components for incubating a beast; it will still cost you a Kubrow Egg or 10 Kavat Genetic Code on top of the imprints. Players can carry up to a maximum of 200 stored imprints. Market and Manufacturing The blueprint can be bought for 50,000 Credits 50,000 and is re-usable indefinitely on all platforms. A crafted Genetic Code Template can also be bought on the Market for 15 Platinum 15. Manufacturing Requirements 10,000 700 300 1 Time: 6 hrs Rush: 5 Platinum 5 Market Price: 15 Blueprints Price: 50,000 Imprinting Genetic Information A Genetic Code Template with an imprint When constructed, Genetic Code Templates are empty. Tenno can use them to store the genetic information of an active pet anytime after it has matured. Imprinting a single genetic code for a pet will take 1.5 hours, but can be rushed for 10 Platinum 10. Up to three imprints can be taken of a single Kavat, and up to two of a single Kubrow. Note that your pet will be unusable while imprinting them, but are usable as normal after the process is over and the finished imprint has been claimed from the incubator. Stored Information The traits mentioned below are stored by a Genetic Code template, with each trait detail having a 50%-per-template weighting when used to create a descending pet.[1] Bear in mind that the information stored will only refer to the pet's original appearance, free of any changes to its coat colors or pattern. Kubrows Kavats Coat Colors (Primary, Secondary, Tertiary) Coat Pattern (Patchy, Hound, Lotus etc.) Eye Color Breed (Sahasa, Raksa, Huras, Sunika, or Chesa) Build (Skinny, Athletic, Bulky) Coat Colors (Primary, Secondary, Tertiary, Accents) Coat Pattern (Hyacinth, Vasca) Eye Color Breed (Smeeta, Adarza, or Vasca) Head Type Tail Type For details regarding which patterns and colors occur naturally, please refer to Kubrow Cosmetics or Kavat Cosmetics. Template Identification A Genetic Code Template contains the name of the pet it was created from and an icon indicating which type of pet it is. Huras KubrowProwling stance. Reflecting that the Huras is orientated around stalking. Raksa KubrowSitting in a loyal stoic pose. Reflecting Raksa specialty of protecting their master. Sahasa KubrowDigging pose. Indicating Sahasa's role in digging up items Sunika KubrowAggresive pouncing pose. Indicating Sunika's role as an attack Kubrow. Chesa KubrowRetrieving pose. Indicating Chesa's trait of fetching items. Smeeta KavatDashing while turning invisible. Indicating Smeeta's specialty of trickery. Adarza KavatFacing a reflection of itself. Indicating Adarza's specialty of reflection. Vasca KavatFacing forward with its body lowered and tail up. When Breeding A New Pet Go to the Incubator and choose the Breeding tab. Click the Begin Incubation button. Click on the empty imprint slots and add the desired imprints. You must enter two imprints; you cannot begin a combined incubation with only one imprint. You can use imprints from two male or two female specimens, or even two of the same companion; two imprints from different Kubrows or Kavats can be chosen for a chance to combine traits. Kubrow imprints cannot be used for breeding Kavats and vice versa. Click Begin A Combined Incubation and wait 48 hours ( Nutrio Incubator Upgrade Segment reduces this to 24 hours) for your new pet, or rush the incubation for 15 Platinum 15. Trivia The screen of the template bears similarities to the results of a Gel electrophoresis test, which is appropriate given the template's function. Prior to Hotfix 14.0.5 (2014-07-21), imprints weren't a surefire way to determine the result of a Kubrow as recessive genes were taken into account. This has since been changed to remove recessive genes, ensuring the imprints contain all the used information. Also, prior to 14.0.5, there was a feature known as a Kubrow Genetic Scrambler, which randomized the Kubrow's genes in exchange for Platinum, and was removed due to a serious resemblance to a slot machine, something that went against everything DE wanted. With the initial launch of Specters of the Rail, the blueprint was unavailable in the Market. This was later corrected in Hotfix 6. From Update: Specters of the Rail 0.0 (2016-07-08) until Hotfix: Specters of the Rail 0.10 (2016-07-15), it was possible to cross-breed Kavats with Kubrows by combining a Kubrow Genetic Code Template with a Kavat Genetic Code Template, resulting in Kubrows or Kavats that, while otherwise normal, had various visual glitches such as incorrect fur textures or malformed models. Players were allowed to keep the hybrids after Hotfix 10 was deployed, but any Genetic Code Templates from them cannot be used for further breeding. Imprints can be traded between players. Patch History Update 40.0 (2025-10-15) Fixed the Genetic Imprint selection menu list not scaling properly to the ratio of the screen. Update 34.0 (2023-10-18) Updated the Imprints error message to be more accurate: Was: "Cannot make imprints of pets when they have not yet matured. Wait for active pet to become an adult before creating imprint." Now: "Cannot make imprints of pets when they have not yet matured. Mature to an adult before creating imprint." Update 31.5 (2022-04-27) Added a cap of 200 for Companion Imprints. Imprints can also be sold from Inventory for Credits. The cap was added to prevent the game from crashing due to having a large amount of Imprints. If you have over 200 Imprints, you will be required to sell Imprints through your Inventory before creating new Imprints. Fixed crash related to the ‘Browse Genetic Imprints’ screen. Update 26.0 (2019-10-31) Each Kavat can now give you 3 Imprints (up from 2). This will apply to all Kavats that predate this update. If you’ve already used 2, you'll find 1 more ready to go! References

Also, prior to 14.0.5, there was a feature known as a Kubrow Genetic Scrambler, which randomized the Kubrow's genes in exchange for Platinum, and was removed due to a serious resemblance to a slot machine, something that went against everything DE wanted. With the initial launch of Specters of the Rail, the blueprint was unavailable in the Market. This was later corrected in Hotfix 6. From Update: Specters of the Rail 0.0 (2016-07-08) until Hotfix: Specters of the Rail 0.10 (2016-07-15), it was possible to cross-breed Kavats with Kubrows by combining a Kubrow Genetic Code Template with a Kavat Genetic Code Template, resulting in Kubrows or Kavats that, while otherwise normal, had various visual glitches such as incorrect fur textures or malformed models. Players were allowed to keep the hybrids after Hotfix 10 was deployed, but any Genetic Code Templates from them cannot be used for further breeding. Imprints can be traded between players. Patch History Update 40.0 (2025-10-15) Fixed the Genetic Imprint selection menu list not scaling properly to the ratio of the screen. Update 34.0 (2023-10-18) Updated the Imprints error message to be more accurate: Was: "Cannot make imprints of pets when they have not yet matured. Wait for active pet to become an adult before creating imprint." Now: "Cannot make imprints of pets when they have not yet matured. Mature to an adult before creating imprint." Update 31.5 (2022-04-27) Added a cap of 200 for Companion Imprints. Imprints can also be sold from Inventory for Credits. The cap was added to prevent the game from crashing due to having a large amount of Imprints. If you have over 200 Imprints, you will be required to sell Imprints through your Inventory before creating new Imprints. Fixed crash related to the ‘Browse Genetic Imprints’ screen. Update 26.0 (2019-10-31) Each Kavat can now give you 3 Imprints (up from 2). This will apply to all Kavats that predate this update. If you’ve already used 2, you'll find 1 more ready to go!  Jahu Gargoyle Quotes
 Jahu Gargoyle Information Quotes Jahu Gargoyle/Quotes

 “A curious blueprint found in the memory banks of Albrecht Entrati's Pom-2.”

 —Albrecht's Blueprint

 The Jahu Gargoyle is a mysterious statue of unknown nature that was crafted during Whispers in the Walls . Additionally, a new variant of the statue known as the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle is introduced as a Clan Dojo decoration during Operation: Gargoyle's Cry .

 Crafting of the initial Jahu Gargoyle during Whispers in the Walls is titled Albrecht's Blueprint .

• 1 Lore & History

• 1.1 Overview

• 1.2 Albrecht's Curio

• 1.3 Murmur Telemetry Chorus

• 2 Trivia

• 3 Gallery

[ edit | edit source ]

[ edit | edit source ]
 Named after the Requiem word for 'Form', the Jahu Gargoyle appears to be a chimerical amalgamation of the three main members of the Cavia, possessing Fibonacci 's head, Bird 3 's wings and hind talons, and Tagfer 's body and forelimbs. It was presumably designed by Albrecht Entrati before he traveled back to the year 1999 .

[ edit | edit source ]
 Main article: Whispers in the Walls
 To complete The Kalymos Sequence, the Tenno is instructed by Albrecht Entrati to construct the Gargoyle, whose blueprint was stored within his Pom-2 console. After placing it within the Sanctum Anatomica and activating it, the Gargoyle automatically translates the Cavia's incoherent Voidtongue , allowing them to communicate with the Tenno and Loid .

 As the Murmur began to break into and corrupt the Sanctum, the Tenno is prompted to let Albrecht's Grimoire "speak with its own voice" in order to defeat The Fragmented Suzerain . Upon interacting with the Jahu Gargoyle, the Tenno places the book in front of the statue. It then opens up on its own, almost as if the Gargoyle itself is 'reading' the Grimoire.

[ edit | edit source ]
 Main article: Operation: Gargoyle's Cry
 To prevent the Murmur from further pervading reality, Fibonacci has devised a solution to detect their influence. He requests each of the Tenno Clans to construct a Vigile Jahu Gargoyle within their Dojo to create a 'living', system-wide surveillance system known as the Murmur Telemetry Chorus . However, a last-minute re-calibration necessitates the Tenno to hunt down The Fragmented and collect their Curses to ensure the system can warn of both incoming and outgoing threats.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • When crafting the Jahu Gargoyle during the quest, the credit cost and build time appear to create the number ' 1999 '.

• A gargoyle that does not also act as a water spout, like the Jahu Gargoyle itself, is actually called a grotesque. This term is referenced in Operation: Gargoyle's Cry , where the player can spend currency called Grotesque Splinters to purchase items from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle.

[ edit | edit source ]

 •

 The Vigile Jahu Gargoyle, as seen in Operation: Gargoyles Cry

[ edit | edit source ]
 • The name Kesslers is probably a reference to Donald J. Kessler , an American astrophysicist and former NASA scientist known for his studies regarding space debris. In 1978 he coined the term Kessler syndrome , a theoretical scenario in which the density of objects in low Earth orbit is so high that collisions between them could result in even more space debris, fuelling additional collisions and making future space activities near-impossible.

[ edit | edit source ]

 Update 29.10 (2021-03-19)

 • Removed from drop tables.

 We wanted to reduce the number of Resources we introduced in our original Railjack launch. They'll remain in your Inventory as we’ll be looking at possibly reintroducing them at a later date. All Blueprints that required these items have been updated to reflect viable Resources, and respective drops have been swapped to Ticor Plate where appropriate. These Resources have also been removed from Helminths Bile Feeding menu. If you spot any mentions of these Resources that we might have missed please let us know!

 For Tenno who have partially contributed these Resources to Wreckage, the percentage of your contribution will be honoured for the Resource that replaces it. Ex: If you contributed 60% of the needed Copernics for a Carcinnox, that 60% contribution will be carried over when the Blueprints are updated.

 Update 27.0 (2019-12-13)

 • Introduced.

Choose when ready.This article refers to valid content that is currently unavailable to be acquired in the live version of the game.It may return to be available again in a future update or event. “Orokin devices used for navigating the solar winds.” —In-Game Description Kesslers were an uncommon resource that could be found in any Empyrean mission. They were used in researches and upgrading the Railjack. They seemed to drop most commonly around Saturn Proxima. Blueprints Requiring Kesslers Click to view the Blueprints requiring Kesslers Blueprint Type Quantity(Research) Lavan Apoc Mk II Ship Turret 315 Lavan Carcinnox Mk II Ship Turret 315 Lavan Cryophon Mk II Ship Turret 315 Lavan Engines Mk II Ship Component 315 Lavan Photor Mk II Ship Turret 315 Lavan Pulsar Mk II Ship Turret 315 Lavan Reactor Mk II Ship Component 315 Lavan Shield Array Mk II Ship Component 315 Squad Energy Restore (Large) x100 Gear (300) Zetki Apoc Mk II Ship Turret 390 Zetki Carcinnox Mk II Ship Turret 390 Zetki Cryophon Mk II Ship Turret 390 Zetki Engines Mk II Ship Component 390 Zetki Photor Mk II Ship Turret 390 Zetki Pulsar Mk II Ship Turret 390 Zetki Reactor Mk II Ship Component 390 Zetki Shield Array Mk II Ship Component 390 Total 5,640 (+300) Research x1 x3 x10 x30 x100 Last updated: Hotfix 29.9.1 (2021-02-24) Trivia The name Kesslers is probably a reference to Donald J. Kessler, an American astrophysicist and former NASA scientist known for his studies regarding space debris. In 1978 he coined the term Kessler syndrome, a theoretical scenario in which the density of objects in low Earth orbit is so high that collisions between them could result in even more space debris, fuelling additional collisions and making future space activities near-impossible. Patch History Update 29.10 (2021-03-19) Removed from drop tables. We wanted to reduce the number of Resources we introduced in our original Railjack launch. They'll remain in your Inventory as we’ll be looking at possibly reintroducing them at a later date. All Blueprints that required these items have been updated to reflect viable Resources, and respective drops have been swapped to Ticor Plate where appropriate. These Resources have also been removed from Helminths Bile Feeding menu. If you spot any mentions of these Resources that we might have missed please let us know! For Tenno who have partially contributed these Resources to Wreckage, the percentage of your contribution will be honoured for the Resource that replaces it. Ex: If you contributed 60% of the needed Copernics for a Carcinnox, that 60% contribution will be carried over when the Blueprints are updated. Update 27.0 (2019-12-13) Introduced.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • Oxylus 's Botanist will automatically loot plants within range.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • The plant's codex description references how Pitcher plants prey on insects (and in some cases, shrews) by luring them into its "pitcher", whose inner surface is slippery, which causes any unfortunate animal to fall into a pool of digestive juices and drown. It is unknown if the Lunar Pitcher has any biological relation to the Dusklight Sarracenia .

• Two overgrown Lunar Pitchers can be found in the Helminth Room.

• There can sometimes spawn a Lunar Pitcher in the extraction tile that is only barely scannable since the terrain almost completely covers it. Two small portions of it are scannable from two different angles.

[ edit | edit source ]
 • If you spawn on the tile-set that starts you outside with a broken bridge above you requiring you to go around the right to enter the base, the Lunar Pitcher will not spawn. Quickly complete the objective and try again. As the tile-set is random each time, this can quickly frustrate players who spawn on this bugged tile-set multiple times in a row rendering The Silver Grove quest line impossible to complete until it changes. (Fixed in Update 25.7 (2019-08-29))

[ edit | edit source ]

 Update 39.0 (2025-06-25)

 • Players will no longer have to scan plants to earn their extracts.
 • Now, plants like Moonlight Dragonlily and Ruk’s Claw will have a “Hold to Collect” context action to pick them up in-mission.

• Oxylus’ Botanist Precept has been changed to automatically pick any plants within 50m over 2s (at max rank).

• This also applies to the Silver Grove Quest, which requires players to collect certain plant resources to progress. The objective descriptions have been updated to specify “gathering” instead of “scanning”.

• The scanning mechanic was overly complicated for new players, so this simplification should benefit Plant Extract gathering for Tenno across the board.

 Update 25.7 (2019-08-29)

 • Fixed issue with Lunar Pitchers not spawning in certain areas in the Orokin Moon tileset.

It is unknown if the Lunar Pitcher has any biological relation to the Dusklight Sarracenia. Two overgrown Lunar Pitchers can be found in the Helminth Room. There can sometimes spawn a Lunar Pitcher in the extraction tile that is only barely scannable since the terrain almost completely covers it. Two small portions of it are scannable from two different angles. Bugs If you spawn on the tile-set that starts you outside with a broken bridge above you requiring you to go around the right to enter the base, the Lunar Pitcher will not spawn. Quickly complete the objective and try again. As the tile-set is random each time, this can quickly frustrate players who spawn on this bugged tile-set multiple times in a row rendering The Silver Grove quest line impossible to complete until it changes. (Fixed in Update 25.7 (2019-08-29)) Media Lunar Pitcher in the Codex. A Lunar Pitcher spawn, inside one of the white plants on the Moon tileset. 2 Lunar Pitchers, in a side room on the Moon tileset. Another Lunar Pitcher spawn, underneath extraction on the Moon tileset. Another Lunar Pitcher spawn, at the base of one of the white trees on the Moon tileset. The objective descriptions have been updated to specify “gathering” instead of “scanning”. Update 25.7 (2019-08-29) Fixed issue with Lunar Pitchers not spawning in certain areas in the Orokin Moon tileset. Update: The Silver Grove (2016-08-19) Introduced.

 Moonlight Threshcone in the Codex.

[ edit | edit source ]

 Update 39.0 (2025-06-25)

 • Players will no longer have to scan plants to earn their extracts.
 • Now, plants like Moonlight Dragonlily and Ruk’s Claw will have a “Hold to Collect” context action to pick them up in-mission.

• Oxylus’ Botanist Precept has been changed to automatically pick any plants within 50m over 2s (at max rank).

• This also applies to the Silver Grove Quest, which requires players to collect certain plant resources to progress. The objective descriptions have been updated to specify “gathering” instead of “scanning”.

• The scanning mechanic was overly complicated for new players, so this simplification should benefit Plant Extract gathering for Tenno across the board.

 Update 11.5 (2013-12-19)

 • Introduced via The Cicero Crisis .

Glowing parts) Activity: Vome Processed Vome Residue required Maximum Points: 8 Rarity: Rare Requires: Friends to start spawning (Rank 4 Entrati Standing) Tradable Basic Adorned Magnificent Benign Infested Tumor x2 Tubercular Gill System x4 Cranial Foremount x2 Benign Infested Tumor x4 Tubercular Gill System x6 Cranial Foremount x2 Benign Infested Tumor x6 Tubercular Gill System x10 Cranial Foremount x2 Trivia Myxostomata, Ostimyr and Cryptosuctus respectively appear to be the pure, hybrid and fully infested version of the same creature. Myxostomata can be found in caves near Infested Seraglio. Myxostomata Trophy “A trophy of a prized catch.” —In-Game Description Myxostomata Trophy is a Fishing Trophy Decorations that can be crafted and displayed in the player's Orbiter. The blueprint can be purchased from Daughter for 10,000 Standing 10,000, requiring the rank of Family with the Entrati.
